untranslatable_reason: Signal requires Ornstein-Uhlenbeck model fitting and calculation of mean-reversion theta coefficient via OLS regression, which are not available in the allowed indicator set. The signal also requires pair-spread calculation (GLD-GDX) and dynamic half-life computation per security, neither of which are supported. Used zscore_20 as a proxy for mean-reversion detection, but this does not capture the full statistical rigor of the original rule.
translation_notes: CHAN_QT_DRY_0 is fundamentally a statistical arbitrage signal requiring custom model fitting. The entry/exit logic approximates mean-reversion using z-scores (deviation from 20-period mean), treating extreme deviations as reversal opportunities. Hold duration capped at 10 days per original parameters. No stop-loss specified (AUTHOR_SILENT). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT because the signal can be applied long or short depending on spread direction.
